title: lolium temulentum subvar. speciosum
intro: Lolium temulentum subvar. speciosum, also known as Darnel, is a subvariety of the Lolium temulentum plant, a type of grass that contains a toxic compound called ergine. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Lolium temulentum subvar. speciosum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and spasms, and act as a diuretic, making it a potential remedy for urinary issues. However, due to its toxicity, the plant is typically used in herbal preparations in small quantities, often as a tincture or infusion, under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
